questions.scenario: A vaulted variable file is committed to Git. During a playbook run, a task renders the secret into `/etc/default/devpolaris-orders-api`. Which statement is most accurate?

options.text: Vault protects the value at rest in the repository, but the decrypted value still exists during the run and can leak through logs or rendered files.
Vault means the secret can never appear on a managed host.
Vault removes the need for file permissions on the rendered destination.
Vault encrypts every Ansible log automatically, even without `no_log`.

questions.explanation: Vault is an at-rest protection for stored variable files. Runtime output, rendered files, and destination permissions still need careful handling.
